description: Mundane English seeds for testing whether steering produces ontology drift from ordinary objects rather than literary or magic-realist vocabulary attractors.
seeds: The receipt on the counter
A plastic folder in the drawer
The bus was ten minutes late
A blue mug near the sink
The spreadsheet was still open
The elevator button was cracked
A delivery label on a cardboard box
I am opening the fridge
I am waiting for the printer
The phone charger under the desk
A grocery list beside the kettle
The laundry basket by the door
A parking ticket on the windshield
The calendar invite was declined
A stapler beside the keyboard
The takeout container in the office fridge
A reusable bag hanging from a chair
The train announcement repeated twice
A paper cup near the vending machine
The apartment key on the windowsill
A cracked tile in the hallway
The remote control under the sofa
A password note folded in a wallet
The checkout line moved slowly
A dentist appointment on the calendar
The sink was still dripping
A bus card in a coat pocket
The printer tray was empty
A roll of tape in the drawer
The email draft had no subject
A supermarket receipt in the rain
The office light would not turn on
A lunchbox left on the bench
The hallway smelled like floor cleaner
A cracked phone screen on the table
The package was marked fragile
A single sock behind the washing machine
The thermostat blinked at midnight
A pencil shaving on the notebook
The vending machine kept the coin
